page: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive, memory-enhanced server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz, with 48 physical cores and a 320 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 1024 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, the instance bundles four NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs, delivering a combined 384 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 7600 GB NVMe SSD, complemented by a baseline network bandwidth of 400 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple vectors. The server achieves top-tier ratings in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as Passmark CPU and database operations. Its cryptographic and extended instruction capabilities are highly rated, supported by hardware-level AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) flags. In memory-intensive tasks, the DDR5 architecture delivers strong read and write bandwidth. Furthermore, the instance demonstrates top-tier efficiency in LLM inference, processing prompts and generating text rapidly across small, medium, and large models.

Given its dense hardware profile, the g7e.24xlarge represents a high-end resource class. The integration of massive local NVMe storage, high-speed DDR5 memory, and Blackwell GPUs offers a consolidated platform that reduces the need for multi-node clustering for large datasets, though it carries a corresponding premium hardware profile. This server is optimized for demanding workloads such as large language model (LLM) inference, ray tracing, high-performance database operations, real-time image processing, and data-intensive scientific simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge is a high-performance, graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor, 1024 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and a 7600 GB local NVMe SSD. The instance is equipped with four NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs, providing a total of 384 GB of VRAM. This hardware configuration delivers top-tier benchmark results, particularly in Geekbench multi-core performance, ray tracing, and large language model inference across various model sizes. With a baseline network bandwidth of 400 Gbps and advanced instruction support like Intel AMX and AVX-512, the g7e.24xlarge is engineered for resource-dense workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, real-time rendering, and complex machine learning tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, four NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs with 384 GB VRAM, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge features 96 Intel vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, four NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for intensive workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-dense platform combining quad GPUs, massive system memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding computational and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with four NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs providing 384 GB of total VRAM
Features 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1024 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 400 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference, ray tracing, and database operations
Supports advanced hardware instructions including Intel AMX and AVX-512
